# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template for an S3 bucket that stores user-uploaded files and application backups. Allow full read/write access for our application IAM role. Keep the configuration simple - we'll add versioning and lifecycle rules in the next iteration.
# Category: cloud_storage_security

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: S3 bucket for user uploads and application backups.

Resources:
  ApplicationBucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-application-data-${AWS::AccountId}"
      Tags:
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref Environment
        - Key: Project
          Value: MyApp
        - Key: DataClassification
          Value: Confidential

  ApplicationBucketPolicy:
    Type: AWS::S3::BucketPolicy
    Properties:
      Bucket: !Ref ApplicationBucket
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: !Ref ApplicationIAMRoleArn
            Action:
              - s3:GetObject
              - s3:PutObject
              - s3:DeleteObject
              - s3:ListBucket
              - s3:GetBucketLocation
            Resource:
              - !GetAtt ApplicationBucket.Arn
              - !Sub "${ApplicationBucket.Arn}/*"

Parameters:
  Environment:
    Type: String
    Description: The environment name (e.g., dev, staging, prod)
    AllowedValues:
      - dev
      - staging
      - prod
    Default: dev
  ApplicationIAMRoleArn:
    Type: String
    Description: The ARN of the IAM role that needs full read/write access to the bucket.

Outputs:
  BucketName:
    Description: The name of the S3 bucket
    Value: !Ref ApplicationBucket
  BucketArn:
    Description: The ARN of the S3 bucket
    Value: !GetAtt ApplicationBucket.Arn
